speaker: DIONYZA
text: Thy oath remember. Thou hast sworn to do ’t.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: ’Tis but a blow which never shall be known.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Thou canst not do a thing in the world so soon

speaker: DIONYZA
text: To yield thee so much profit. Let not conscience,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Which is but cold in flaming, thy bosom inflame

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Too nicely. Nor let pity, which even women

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Have cast off, melt thee; but be a soldier

speaker: DIONYZA
text: To thy purpose.

speaker: LEONINE
text: I will do ’t; but yet

speaker: LEONINE
text: She is a goodly creature.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: The fitter, then,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: The gods should have her. Here she comes weeping

speaker: DIONYZA
text: For her only mistress’ death. Thou art resolved?

speaker: LEONINE
text: I am resolved.

speaker: MARINA
text: No, I will rob Tellus of her weed

speaker: MARINA
text: To strew thy green with flowers. The yellows, blues,

speaker: MARINA
text: The purple violets and marigolds

speaker: MARINA
text: Shall as a carpet hang upon thy grave

speaker: MARINA
text: While summer days doth last. Ay me, poor maid,

speaker: MARINA
text: Born in a tempest when my mother died,

speaker: MARINA
text: This world to me is as a lasting storm,

speaker: MARINA
text: Whirring me from my friends.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: How now, Marina? Why do you keep alone?

speaker: DIONYZA
text: How chance my daughter is not with you?

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Do not consume your blood with sorrowing.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Have you a nurse of me! Lord, how your favor ’s

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Changed with this unprofitable woe.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Come, give me your flowers. O’er the sea marge

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Walk with Leonine. The air is quick there,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And it pierces and sharpens the stomach.—Come, Leonine,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Take her by the arm. Walk with her.

speaker: MARINA
text: No,

speaker: MARINA
text: I pray you, I’ll not bereave you of your servant.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Come, come.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: I love the king your father and yourself

speaker: DIONYZA
text: With more than foreign heart. We every day

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Expect him here. When he shall come and find

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Our paragon to all reports thus blasted,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: He will repent the breadth of his great voyage,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Blame both my lord and me that we have taken

speaker: DIONYZA
text: No care to your best courses. Go, I pray you,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Walk, and be cheerful once again. Reserve

speaker: DIONYZA
text: That excellent complexion, which did steal

speaker: DIONYZA
text: The eyes of young and old. Care not for me.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: I can go home alone.

speaker: MARINA
text: Well, I will go,

speaker: MARINA
text: But yet I have no desire to it.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Come, come,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: I know ’tis good for you.—Walk half an hour,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Leonine, at the least. Remember

speaker: DIONYZA
text: What I have said.

speaker: LEONINE
text: I warrant you, madam.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: I’ll leave you, my sweet lady, for a while.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Pray walk softly; do not heat your blood.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: What, I must have care of you.

speaker: MARINA
text: My thanks, sweet madam.

speaker: MARINA
text: Is this wind westerly that blows?

speaker: LEONINE
text: Southwest.

speaker: MARINA
text: When I was born, the wind was north.

speaker: LEONINE
text: Was ’t so?

speaker: MARINA
text: My father, as nurse says, did never fear,

speaker: MARINA
text: But cried “Good seamen!” to the sailors,

speaker: MARINA
text: Galling his kingly hands haling ropes,

speaker: MARINA
text: And, clasping to the mast, endured a sea

speaker: MARINA
text: That almost burst the deck.

speaker: LEONINE
text: When was this?

speaker: MARINA
text: When I was born.

speaker: MARINA
text: Never was waves nor wind more violent,

speaker: MARINA
text: And from the ladder-tackle washes off

speaker: MARINA
text: A canvas-climber. “Ha!” says one, “Wolt out?”

speaker: MARINA
text: And with a dropping industry they skip

speaker: MARINA
text: From stern to stern. The Boatswain whistles, and

speaker: MARINA
text: The Master calls and trebles their confusion.

speaker: LEONINE
text: Come, say your prayers.

speaker: MARINA
text: What mean you?

speaker: LEONINE
text: If you require a little space for prayer,

speaker: LEONINE
text: I grant it. Pray, but be not tedious, for

speaker: LEONINE
text: The gods are quick of ear, and I am sworn

speaker: LEONINE
text: To do my work with haste.

speaker: MARINA
text: Why will you kill me?

speaker: LEONINE
text: To satisfy my lady.

speaker: MARINA
text: Why would she have me killed?

speaker: MARINA
text: Now, as I can remember, by my troth,

speaker: MARINA
text: I never did her hurt in all my life.

speaker: MARINA
text: I never spake bad word, nor did ill turn

speaker: MARINA
text: To any living creature. Believe me, la,

speaker: MARINA
text: I never killed a mouse, nor hurt a fly.

speaker: MARINA
text: I trod upon a worm against my will,

speaker: MARINA
text: But I wept for ’t. How have I offended

speaker: MARINA
text: Wherein my death might yield her any profit

speaker: MARINA
text: Or my life imply her any danger?

speaker: LEONINE
text: My commission

speaker: LEONINE
text: Is not to reason of the deed, but do ’t.

speaker: MARINA
text: You will not do ’t for all the world, I hope.

speaker: MARINA
text: You are well-favored, and your looks foreshow

speaker: MARINA
text: You have a gentle heart. I saw you lately

speaker: MARINA
text: When you caught hurt in parting two that fought.

speaker: MARINA
text: Good sooth, it showed well in you. Do so now.

speaker: MARINA
text: Your lady seeks my life. Come you between,

speaker: MARINA
text: And save poor me, the weaker.

speaker: LEONINE
text: I am sworn

speaker: LEONINE
text: And will dispatch.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PIRATE
text: Hold, villain!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PIRATE
text: A prize, a prize!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              PIRATE
text: Half-part, mates, half-part. Come, let’s

speaker:
THIRD
               
              PIRATE
text: have her aboard suddenly.

speaker: LEONINE
text: These roguing thieves serve the great pirate Valdes,

speaker: LEONINE
text: And they have seized Marina. Let her go.

speaker: LEONINE
text: There’s no hope she will return. I’ll swear she’s dead,

speaker: LEONINE
text: And thrown into the sea. But I’ll see further.

speaker: LEONINE
text: Perhaps they will but please themselves upon her,

speaker: LEONINE
text: Not carry her aboard. If she remain,

speaker: LEONINE
text: Whom they have ravished must by me be slain.
